prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.66) and extreme seasonality capture variance (0.90), with THETA leading trend tracking while CWT_rf dominates seasonal correlation. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, requiring robust combination methods that handle both trend and seasonality heterogeneity. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating the high disagreement between models.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "THETA",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "NaiveSeasonal", "late": "DWT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ETS", "CWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "FT_rf", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["svr", "FT_catboost", "rf", "FT_svr", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6612,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.66) and extreme seasonality capture variance (0.90), with THETA leading trend tracking while CWT_rf dominates seasonal correlation. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, requiring robust combination methods that handle both trend and seasonality heterogeneity. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating the high disagreement between models."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.66 (best ONLY_CWT_catboost at 284.5 vs worst CWT_svr at 505.4) justifies rmse_focus preset. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase-shifted predictions and high seasonality variance (0.90). best_per_horizon_by_validation captures trend champions, while topk_mean_k5 provides robust selection with shrinkage. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds weighted diversity. exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 complements with exponential decay weighting."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all methods use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Score margin between top candidates is small (0.0147). Universe leaderboard shows best_per_horizon_by_validation already in set, and exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim variants have lower scores (0.7652) than current weighted candidates. No redundancy - 5 candidates with ≥2 different types is acceptable.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.66) and extreme seasonality capture variance (0.90), with THETA leading trend tracking while CWT_rf dominates seasonal correlation. The true series exhibits mixed trend directions across folds, requiring robust combination methods that handle both trend and seasonality heterogeneity. DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths while mitigating the high disagreement between models.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring diverse methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.66 (best ONLY_CWT_catboost at 284.5 vs worst CWT_svr at 505.4) justifies rmse_focus preset. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase-shifted predictions and high seasonality variance (0.90). best_per_horizon_by_validation captures trend champions, while topk_mean_k5 provides robust selection with shrinkage. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds weighted diversity. exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0 complements with exponential decay weighting.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - all methods use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Score margin between top candidates is small (0.0147). Universe leaderboard shows best_per_horizon_by_validation already in set, and exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim variants have lower scores (0.7652) than current weighted candidates. No redundancy - 5 candidates with ≥2 different types is acceptable.
